title: The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
subhead: The Israeli human rights organization B'Tselem documented the deaths of 54 minors killed by the Israeli army in the occupied West Bank in January 2025 and the impunity enjoyed by its soldiers.

European lawmakers call on FIFA to investigate 'Peace Prize' awarded to Trump by Infantino
subhead: In a letter sent Monday to the president and ethics committee of football's governing body, around 50 MEPs request clarification regarding the trophy awarded to the US President in 2025.

title: US Supreme Court upholds Trump sexual assault judgment
subhead: The Supreme Court rejected Donald Trump's bid to overturn a judgment that he sexually assaulted and defamed author E. Jean Carroll, to whom he was ordered to pay $5 million in damages for sexual assault and defamation.

title: Macron brings back viral aviator sunglasses
subhead: At a ceremony with the Sultan of Oman, President Emmanuel Macron once again sported his now-iconic aviator sunglasses, which he debuted at the Davos World Economic Forum in January, in a speech featuring the English phrase 'for sure.'

title: 250 years of American independence: Massacre on the prairie
subhead: 'America 250' (8/13). While US Army troops and those of the Confederacy fought each other, another conflict was taking place on the plains of the American West, against Native Americans. In November 1864, at Sand Creek, in what became Colorado in 1876, the thirst for gold and land drove settlers to barbarism.

French research thrown into turmoil by a multifaceted crisis
subhead: France's scientific community faces a host of challenges, from dwindling financial resources, ideological attacks and the emergence of artificial intelligence. The result is worrying for researchers nationwide, at a time when France is also slipping behind in scientific publishing.

title: 'Their mouths, eyes, hands, were taped and they were thrown into the hole': Russian soldiers confront Putin with reality of front lines
subhead: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.

title: Musée d'Orsay faces demand for restitution of Van Gogh painting
subhead: The descendant of a German Jewish art collector persecuted by the Nazis is seeking the return of the painting 'Hôpital Saint-Paul à Saint-Rémy-de-Provence.' The Commission for the Compensation of Victims of Spoliation is expected to review the case starting in September.
